---
pagetitle: "Information dynamics in human–AI systems"
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

```{=html}

<style>
.research-subpage {
  --research-accent: #426b73;
  --research-accent-soft: #e7f0f1;
  --research-ink: #1f2933;
  --research-muted: #5f6f7a;
  --research-line: #d7e1e4;
  --research-bg: #ffffff;
}

.research-subpage-intro,
.research-subpage-section {
  max-width: 1120px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 3.5rem 1.5rem;
}

.research-subpage-intro {
  padding-top: 2rem;
  padding-bottom: 2.5rem;
}

.research-kicker {
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.14em;
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  color: var(--research-accent);
  font-weight: 700;
  margin-bottom: 0.9rem;
}

.research-title {
  font-size: clamp(2.2rem, 5vw, 4rem);
  line-height: 0.98;
  margin: 0 0 1.15rem;
  color: var(--research-ink);
  font-weight: 800;
  letter-spacing: -0.04em;
}

.research-lede {
  max-width: 900px;
  font-size: 1.12rem;
  line-height: 1.75;
  color: var(--research-ink);
}

.research-muted {
  color: var(--research-muted);
}

.research-band {
  background: #f7fafb;
  border-top: 1px solid var(--research-line);
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--research-line);
}

.research-section-title {
  font-size: clamp(1.6rem, 3vw, 2.3rem);
  margin: 0 0 1.2rem;
  color: var(--research-ink);
}

.research-subpage-section p,
.research-subpage-section li {
  line-height: 1.65;
}

.demo-button,
.publication-links a {
  font-weight: 650;
  text-decoration: none;
  color: var(--research-accent);
}

.demo-button {
  display: inline-block;
  margin-top: 0.6rem;
  padding: 0.7rem 1rem;
  border: 1px solid var(--research-accent);
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: var(--research-accent-soft);
}

.publication-list {
  display: grid;
  gap: 1.25rem;
}

.publication-item {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(160px, 0.28fr) minmax(0, 0.72fr);
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: start;
  background: var(--research-bg);
  border: 1px solid var(--research-line);
  border-radius: 18px;
  padding: 1rem;
  box-shadow: 0 8px 22px rgba(31, 41, 51, 0.05);
}

.publication-snapshot {
  width: 100%;
  aspect-ratio: 4 / 3;
  object-fit: cover;
  border-radius: 12px;
  background: var(--research-accent-soft);
  border: 1px solid var(--research-line);
}

.publication-detail h3 {
  font-size: 1.12rem;
  margin: 0 0 0.35rem;
  color: var(--research-ink);
}

.publication-meta {
  color: var(--research-muted);
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  line-height: 1.55;
  margin-bottom: 0.65rem;
}

.publication-description {
  color: var(--research-ink);
  line-height: 1.65;
  margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
}

@media (max-width: 720px) {
  .research-title {
    font-size: clamp(2.2rem, 12vw, 3.2rem);
  }

  .publication-item {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }

  .publication-snapshot {
    max-width: 420px;
  }
}
</style>


<div class="research-subpage">

<section class="research-subpage-intro">
  <div class="research-kicker">Generative AI · Feedback · Adaptation</div>
  <h2 class="research-title">Information dynamics in human–AI systems</h2>
  <div class="research-lede">
    <p>How does information evolve when humans and AI systems participate in feedback loops?</p>
  </div>
</section>


<section class="research-subpage-section">
  <h2 class="research-section-title">Overview</h2>
  <p>
    This research theme examines how information evolves when humans and AI systems participate in feedback loops. I am interested in generative AI not only as a technical artefact, but as a latent field shaping cultural, organisational, and epistemic dynamics.
  </p>
  <p>
    Cybernetics offers a practical language for studying these systems: communication, feedback, adaptation, observation, and recursive change.
  </p>
</section>



<section class="research-band">
  <div class="research-subpage-section">
    <h2 class="research-section-title">Selected publications</h2>
    <p class="research-muted">
      This list is intended for publications connected to human–AI interaction, generative AI, communication, feedback, adaptation, and information dynamics. Replace each placeholder with a relevant publication for this research theme.
    </p>

    <div class="publication-list">
      <article class="publication-item">
        <img class="publication-snapshot" src="../images/publications/information-dynamics-paper-01.jpg" alt="Snapshot image for a publication related to this research theme">
        <div class="publication-detail">
          <h3>Publication title placeholder</h3>
          <div class="publication-meta">
            <strong>Authors:</strong> Author One, Author Two, Sungyeon Hong<br>
            <strong>Year:</strong> 20XX<br>
            <strong>Publisher / venue:</strong> Journal or conference name
          </div>
          <p class="publication-description">
            Add a brief description of the paper here. Focus on the core question, method, system studied, and how the work contributes to this research theme.
          </p>
          <div class="publication-links">
            <a href="https://doi.org/xx.xxxx/example-doi">DOI link →</a>
          </div>
        </div>
      </article>

      <article class="publication-item">
        <img class="publication-snapshot" src="../images/publications/information-dynamics-paper-02.jpg" alt="Snapshot image for another publication related to this research theme">
        <div class="publication-detail">
          <h3>Publication title placeholder</h3>
          <div class="publication-meta">
            <strong>Authors:</strong> Author One, Author Two, Sungyeon Hong<br>
            <strong>Year:</strong> 20XX<br>
            <strong>Publisher / venue:</strong> Journal or conference name
          </div>
          <p class="publication-description">
            Add a short, reader-friendly summary that explains why this publication matters and how it connects to the larger research agenda.
          </p>
          <div class="publication-links">
            <a href="https://doi.org/xx.xxxx/example-doi">DOI link →</a>
          </div>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>


</div>

```
